meta_title: Impact Doors in Boynton Beach, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Boynton Beach, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind rated, in-house install. Free quotes & $0 down financing.
h1: Impact Doors Built for Boynton Beach's Coastal Reality
intro:
Boynton Beach sits just two miles from the Atlantic coast, a location that delivers stunning ocean breezes, easy access to Boynton Beach Inlet, and some of the most dynamic weather conditions in all of Palm Beach County. For homeowners in neighborhoods like Marina Village, Indian Spring, Leisureville, and the Aberdeen community, that coastal proximity is part of the appeal — but it also means your home faces wind, rain, and storm surge threats that demand serious, code-compliant protection. Impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade for Boynton Beach homes; they're a critical investment in the safety of your family and the long-term value of a property averaging $380,000 in today's market.

With more than half of all Boynton Beach homes — approximately 52% — built before Florida's landmark 1994 building code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew's devastation, a large portion of this city's housing stock was constructed during an era when wind resistance standards were far less stringent. That means older entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors throughout neighborhoods along the Congress Avenue Corridor and beyond may be doing little more than providing a visual barrier against the storms that periodically target South Florida. A well-designed impact door, by contrast, is engineered to withstand the intense pressure differentials, flying debris, and wind-driven rain that accompany major hurricane events — keeping your home's envelope sealed even when a storm rages outside.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped more than 500 South Florida homeowners upgrade their homes with certified impact-resistant products. Our team understands the specific challenges that Boynton Beach properties face — from salt air corrosion near the Intracoastal Waterway to the unique structural considerations of concrete block and frame homes built across different eras. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door or upgrading every opening on your home with impact-rated doors, we bring the expertise, the right brands, and a fully in-house installation team to every project we take on in this city.

section_why.h2: Why Boynton Beach Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When you're investing in impact doors for a home in Boynton Beach, the company you hire matters as much as the products you choose. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on a simple but powerful promise: exceptional service at every step, from your first consultation through the final inspection. We call ourselves 'The Kings of Service,' and that title reflects a commitment to doing things right — not just fast.

One of the most important distinctions that separates Premier Impact Windows & Roofing from many competitors operating in the Boynton Beach area is our 100% in-house installation model. We don't subcontract your project to a rotating cast of third-party crews. Every door we install is handled by trained, experienced technicians who work directly for us, who are accountable to our quality standards, and who understand the specific installation requirements that Palm Beach County inspectors look for. With more than 60 years of combined experience across our team, we've seen virtually every installation challenge that older Boynton Beach homes can present — from out-of-square rough openings in mid-century construction to the unique framing scenarios found in communities like Leisureville's active-adult residences.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and our status as a fully licensed and insured contractor in Florida aren't just credentials we display — they're the result of a consistent, disciplined approach to every job. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you'll work with knowledgeable professionals who will assess your home's specific openings, explain your product options in plain language, help you understand what to expect during the City of Boynton Beach permitting process, and stand behind the work after installation is complete. We handle the permit applications, coordinate inspections, and make sure your project closes out cleanly with the City of Boynton Beach Building Division — because a permitted and inspected installation isn't just code compliance, it's proof your home improvement was done correctly, which matters enormously when it comes time to sell or file a wind mitigation report.

section_history.h2: A City That Knows Hurricanes: Boynton Beach's Storm History and What It Means for Your Doors
section_history.body:
Boynton Beach has weathered nine significant named storms since 1990, a track record that serves as a constant reminder of South Florida's vulnerability to Atlantic hurricane activity. Each of those storms left behind lessons — some learned the hard way by homeowners whose entry points failed under pressure. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the event that rewrote the rules for Florida construction, but its most catastrophic impacts fell south of Boynton Beach in Miami-Dade County. It was the storms that followed — Frances and Jeanne in 2004, and Wilma in 2005 — that delivered painful, direct blows to Palm Beach County communities, exposing the vulnerabilities of homes that lacked wind-rated door systems.

Hurricane Wilma in particular struck Palm Beach County with sustained winds that caused widespread damage throughout Boynton Beach, stripping roofs, shattering glass, and driving water through failed door and window systems. The storm's rapid intensification and its broad damage swath reminded coastal communities that even hurricanes that make landfall elsewhere can produce catastrophic conditions. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced that reality when it tracked up the spine of Florida, affecting Boynton Beach with tropical-storm-to-hurricane-force wind gusts that rattled unprotected openings throughout residential neighborhoods.

The pattern is clear: Boynton Beach is not a city that can afford to leave its door openings to chance. Every storm season that passes without a major direct hit is a reprieve, not a guarantee. Impact doors represent the most effective and permanent solution to the vulnerability that ordinary entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors create. Unlike hurricane shutters that must be deployed and stored, impact doors are always protecting your home — even when a storm develops faster than forecasted.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Palm Beach County Permit Requirements for Impact Doors in Boynton Beach
section_code.body:
Boynton Beach falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction for building code enforcement, operating under the Florida Building Code (FBC) as enforced by the City of Boynton Beach Building Division. While Boynton Beach is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — this does not mean impact door requirements are relaxed. Palm Beach County's coastal proximity and its classification within a 170 mph wind speed design zone mean that all replacement doors must still meet rigorous hurricane-resistant standards, including impact testing protocols that verify performance against windborne debris and pressure cycling.

Under Florida Building Code requirements applicable to Boynton Beach, replacement doors in homes must either be impact-rated products with valid FBC product approval numbers, or existing openings must be protected by code-compliant shutters or other approved systems. For most homeowners, impact doors are the preferred permanent solution because they meet these requirements without the inconvenience of secondary protection systems. Products installed in Boynton Beach must carry documented compliance with ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996 testing standards, which simulate windborne debris impact and cyclic pressure loads equivalent to major hurricane conditions.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process with the City of Boynton Beach Building Division on your behalf. We prepare and submit the permit application, provide all required product approval documentation, and schedule the final inspection. This matters because unpermitted door replacements can create serious complications — from voided homeowner's insurance policies to required removal-and-reinstallation during future sales. Our in-house team ensures that every installation we complete is fully documented, inspected, and on record with the city, giving you the peace of mind that your investment is protected from a compliance standpoint.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Matched to Boynton Beach's Coastal Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact-resistant doors from two trusted industry brands — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which offer product lines specifically engineered to perform in South Florida's demanding coastal environment. These aren't standard residential door products with impact glass simply substituted in; they are purpose-built systems that integrate laminated glass panels, reinforced frames, and multi-point locking mechanisms designed to maintain structural integrity when subjected to 170 mph wind loading and debris impact forces.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) impact door products are manufactured with the South Florida market in mind, meeting Florida Building Code product approval requirements and offering a range of styles that suit the diverse architectural character of Boynton Beach homes — from the Mediterranean-influenced residences of Aberdeen and Indian Spring to the more contemporary homes along the Congress Avenue Corridor. CWI impact doors similarly offer exceptional build quality with options for impact-rated entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors. The multi-point locking systems available on both brands are a critical feature: rather than a single deadbolt point, these doors engage locking mechanisms at multiple points along the door slab's edge, dramatically improving resistance to the inward and outward pressure differentials that hurricanes produce.

For Boynton Beach homeowners near the Intracoastal Waterway or within direct sight of the ocean — particularly in Marina Village and coastal corridor properties — corrosion resistance is not a minor consideration. Both ESW and CWI product lines offer frame finishes and hardware components rated for marine-adjacent environments, ensuring that the salt air exposure that degrades conventional door hardware over time won't compromise the performance of your impact door investment. We help every homeowner select the right configuration, glass package, and finish for their specific property.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Boynton Beach — and How to Finance Them Affordably
section_cost.body:
In a Boynton Beach market where the average home value sits around $380,000, impact door replacement is one of the most cost-effective improvements a homeowner can make in terms of both protection value and return on investment. The cost of impact door installation varies based on the number and size of openings, door type (entry, sliding glass, or French), glass package, and frame finish selected. Entry door replacements typically range from approximately $1,500 to $4,000 per unit installed, while larger sliding glass door systems and multi-panel French door configurations can range higher depending on size and complexity. Full-home door replacement projects — covering entry, rear, and side access points — vary widely based on the home's layout and the number of openings involved.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options that make impact door installation accessible for middle-income Boynton Beach households without requiring a significant upfront cash outlay. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. For a family replacing two or three door openings, the monthly financing cost can be remarkably manageable — especially when factored against the insurance savings, energy efficiency improvements, and storm protection value that impact doors deliver from day one.

Beyond the upfront investment, impact doors deliver ongoing financial returns. Wind mitigation inspections that document impact-rated doors at all openings can trigger meaningful premium reductions from homeowner's insurance carriers — reductions that, over a five-to-ten year period, can substantially offset the original installation cost. Additionally, impact doors can improve your home's energy performance by reducing air infiltration, which is particularly relevant during Boynton Beach's long, humid summer months when air conditioning costs are a consistent budget concern. When you factor in protection value, insurance savings, energy savings, and property value enhancement, impact doors are rarely a question of whether — but when.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Boynton Beach Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
Palm Beach County homeowners face some of the highest homeowner's insurance premiums in the United States, a direct reflection of the region's well-documented hurricane risk. For Boynton Beach residents — especially those in older homes built before 1994 code reforms — annual insurance premiums can represent a significant household expense. Impact doors, when properly documented through a wind mitigation inspection, can generate premium credits that produce real, recurring savings year after year.

Florida's wind mitigation credit system, established under legislation designed to incentivize hurricane hardening, rewards homeowners who install impact-rated products at all openings. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents that your home's doors meet FBC standards for windborne debris protection, that information is submitted to your insurance carrier, which is then required by Florida law to apply the corresponding premium reduction. The size of the credit varies by carrier and policy structure, but Boynton Beach homeowners in the coastal wind zone often see meaningful discounts — particularly when impact doors are combined with other improvements like impact windows or a qualifying roof system.

Carriers operating extensively in the Palm Beach County market — including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits for impact-rated door systems. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's fully permitted installations come with documentation packages that support the wind mitigation inspection process, ensuring that your investment translates efficiently into the insurance savings you're entitled to receive. Our team can also advise you on the sequencing of improvements — for example, whether adding impact windows alongside your doors will maximize your mitigation credit in a single inspection — so you can build a protection plan that delivers the best financial return over time.

faqs.q: Do I need impact doors if my Boynton Beach home already has hurricane shutters?
faqs.a: Hurricane shutters are a code-compliant option, but they require manual deployment before a storm and must be stored when not in use. Impact doors provide permanent, year-round protection without any action on your part. For Boynton Beach homeowners who travel frequently, are elderly, or simply want the reliability of always-on protection, impact doors are strongly preferred — and they provide the wind mitigation documentation that can reduce your insurance premiums, which shutters may not fully replicate depending on your policy.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost, and what financing is available for Boynton Beach homeowners?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Boynton Beach typically ranges from around $1,500 to $4,000+ per opening depending on door type, size, and features selected. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants, making it possible to upgrade your home's protection without a large upfront payment. We provide free, no-obligation consultations and transparent pricing so you can plan your project with confidence.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door installation in Boynton Beach?
faqs.a: All impact door replacements in Boynton Beach require a building permit from the City of Boynton Beach Building Division. The permit process involves submitting product approval documentation confirming Florida Building Code compliance, and a final inspection must be completed before the permit is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — application, documentation, and inspection scheduling — so you don't have to navigate the process alone. Never allow a contractor to install impact doors without pulling the required permits.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Boynton Beach home?
faqs.a: The physical installation of impact doors is typically completed in one to two days for most Boynton Beach homes, depending on the number of openings and any site-specific conditions. Permit processing timelines with the City of Boynton Beach Building Division can vary but generally take one to three weeks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates all scheduling and communicates clearly at each stage so you always know where your project stands. We aim to minimize disruption and complete every installation efficiently without compromising quality.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Boynton Beach?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases impact doors contribute to meaningful insurance savings for Boynton Beach homeowners. Once installed and permitted, a wind mitigation inspection can document your impact-rated doors, and Florida law requires your insurance carrier to apply corresponding wind mitigation credits to your policy. The amount varies by carrier and the extent of your home's hurricane hardening, but savings can range from hundreds to over a thousand dollars annually — particularly when impact doors are combined with impact windows or a qualifying roof system.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation impact door consultation — and discover how affordable permanent storm protection can be with financing starting at $87/month and $0 down.
